title: Sutera neglecta
intro: Sutera neglecta, also known as Dusty Miller, is a flowering plant species in the family Plantaginaceae, native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sutera neglecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sutera neglecta include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Sutera neglecta is classified within the family Scrophulariaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as figworts and snapdragons. This family is part of the larger order Lamiales, which includes over 20,000 species of plants. The order Lamiales is further classified within the clade Asterids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families of mints, oreganos, and honeysuckles. Sutera neglecta is native to tropical Africa and is often cultivated as an ornamental plant for its delicate, white flowers. It is a member of the genus Sutera, which includes several other species of flowering plants.
